content.problemStatement:
There is an infinite sheet of paper and a pen. At any time, the pen may be placed above a point (x,y)(x, y)(x,y) on the paper or touching it at a point (x,y)(x, y)(x,y); in both cases, we call the point (x,y)(x, y)(x,y) the position of the pen. The initial position of the pen is (0,0)(0, 0)(0,0).

You may perform the following types of actions:


move the pen down until it touches the paper
lift the pen (if it was touching the paper); after this action, the pen is not touching the paper
move the pen from its current position (x,y)(x, y)(x,y) to (x−1,y)(x-1, y)(x−1,y) or (x+1,y)(x+1, y)(x+1,y)
move the pen from its current position (0,y)(0, y)(0,y) to (0,y+1)(0, y+1)(0,y+1)
Actions of the first two types take no time, an action of the third type takes 111 second and an action of the fourth type takes 111 second. Each action may be performed any number of times. You may only move the pen for at most PPP seconds in total.

Whenever the pen is moved from (x1,y1)(x_1, y_1)(x1​,y1​) to (x2,y2)(x_2, y_2)(x2​,y2​) while touching the paper, it draws a line segment between these points (inclusive). Drawing over a line segment repeatedly is allowed.

You are given NNN special line segments; each of them is parallel with the xxx-axis. A special line segment is fully drawn if each point belonging to it also belongs to some drawn line segment. Your goal is to fully draw as many special line segments as possible in PPP seconds. How many can you fully draw?
constraints: The special line segments do not touch or intersect
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers N and P. Each of the next N lines contains three space-separated integers y, s, and e denoting a special line segment between points (s, y) and (e, y) (including these points).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the maximum number of special segments you can fully draw.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case, we sort the line segments based on their y-coordinates and lengths, and then try to draw each one. If we have enough time to draw a line segment, we draw it and subtract the time it takes to draw it from our total time.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a greedy algorithm. First, we sort the line segments based on their y-coordinates and lengths. Then, we iterate over the sorted line segments and try to draw each one. If we have enough time to draw a line segment, we draw it and subtract the time it takes to draw it from our total time. We keep track of the number of line segments we have drawn and return this number at the end.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N log N) — where T is the number of test cases and N is the number of line segments. The sorting step takes O(N log N) time, and we do this for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — where N is the number of line segments. We need to store the line segments in an array to sort them.

code.Python3:
# COMPLETE, RUNNABLE Python3 code
import sys
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N, P = map(int, input().split())
    lines = []
    for _ in range(N):
        y, s, e = map(int, input().split())
        lines.append((y, s, e))
    lines.sort(key=lambda x: (x[0], x[2] - x[1]))
    count = 0
    for y, s, e in lines:
        time = e - s + (y > 0)
        if P >= time:
            P -= time
            count += 1
    print(count)
